summary: Clear soundboard capture showcases a soaring second set with a legendary Morning Dew, but uneven moments in Stagger Lee and Crazy Fingers temper the excitement.
review: The gd1993‑09‑09 recording is praised for its solid soundboard mix that delivers good clarity, though a few listeners note a lack of punch on certain tracks. Musically the show shines in the second set – especially the emotive Morning Dew, Terrapin Station and Saint of Circumstance – while some fans criticize sloppy vocals and mangled performances of Stagger Lee and Crazy Fingers.

ai_rating.rationale: Weighted analysis (70 % show quality, 30 % recording) yields a solid but not flawless rating; many reviewers award high marks for the performance and soundboard clarity, while several call out notable flaws.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced SBD; a few comments about missing bass on Stagger Lee and occasional lack of punch, but overall considered a solid recording for the era.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is highlighted as the high point with strong transitions and emotional peaks; first set suffers from a few lyric slips and vocal miscues.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Mixed feedback – early lyric stumbles on Here Comes Sunshine and Stagger Lee, but later praised for powerful playing during Morning Dew and overall fire.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted absence of bass on Stagger Lee by some listeners; also credited with “evil thunder sounds” during the Dew.
